content.query:
use nyc_taxi_discovery;

-------------------------- single line JSON ----------------------------
select 
    *
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

select 
    JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type') as payment_type,
    JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc') as payment_type_desc
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

------------------------ declare data type for each column using CAST ------------------------------
select 
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type') as SMALLINT) as payment_type,
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc') as VARCHAR(15)) as payment_type_desc
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

--- json_value has some limitations - 1. it is difficult to define the data types for the column  
--- 2. if you have an array within json document, you can go and pick a perticular specific occurrence of an array but you cant really explode the entire array
--- 3. also not the efficient function
--- Hence synapse provides an alternative called open_json - table valued function which takes an adjacent document & turns its objects & properties into rows & columns
--- It is more efficient than json_value when you are dealing with huge amount of data
--- You can define data types instead of casting , you can list the columns & rename it if required
select 
     *
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f'
    --ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    payment_type_desc VARCHAR(20)
);

select 
     payment_type,
     payment_type_desc
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f'
    --ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    payment_type_desc VARCHAR(20)
);

select 
     payment_type,
     description
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f'
    --ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    description VARCHAR(20) '$.payment_type_desc'
);

--------------------------- Explore JSON Array -----------------------
select 
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type') as SMALLINT) as payment_type,
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc[0].value') as VARCHAR(15)) as payment_type_desc
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type_array.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

select 
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type') as SMALLINT) as payment_type,
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc[0].value') as VARCHAR(15)) as payment_type_desc_0,
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc[1].value') as VARCHAR(15)) as payment_type_desc_1
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type_array.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

----------- Use open_json to explode array 

select 
    *
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type_array.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH
(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    payment_type_desc NVARCHAR(MAX) as json
)
CROSS APPLY openjson(payment_type_desc)
WITH(
    sub_type SMALLINT,
    value VARCHAR(20)
);

select 
    payment_type,
    sub_type,
    value
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type_array.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH
(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    payment_type_desc NVARCHAR(MAX) as json
)
CROSS APPLY openjson(payment_type_desc)
WITH(
    sub_type SMALLINT,
    value VARCHAR(20)
);


---------------------------------------- Query Standard JSON --------------------------
select 
     rate_code_id,
     rate_code
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/rate_code.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as rate_code
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    rate_code_id TINYINT,
    rate_code VARCHAR(20)
);

------------ Query Classic JSON - these are same as standard json when we use serverless sql pool -----------------
select 
     rate_code_id,
     rate_code
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/rate_code_multi_line.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as rate_code
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    rate_code_id TINYINT,
    rate_code VARCHAR(20)
);
